fundamentals.name: Texas Instruments Incorporated
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Texas Instruments Incorporated designs, manufactures, and sells semiconductors to electronics designers and manufacturers in the United States, China, the rest of Asia, Europe, the Middle East, Africa, Japan, and internationally. It operates through Analog and Embedded Processing segments. The Analog segment offers power products to manage power requirements across various voltage levels, including battery-management solutions, DC/DC switching regulators, AC/DC and isolated controllers and converters, power switches, linear regulators, voltage references, multiphase controllers and power stages, and lighting products. This segment also provides signal chain products that sense, condition, and measure real-world signals and convert them into data to be transferred or converted for further processing and control, such as amplifiers, data converters, interface products, motor drives, clocks, and logic and sensing products. The Embedded Processing segment offers microcontrollers, processors, wireless connectivity, and radar products; and applications processors for specific computing activity. It also provides DLP products primarily for use in projecting high-definition images; calculators; and application-specific integrated circuits. Its products are used in various markets, such as industrial, automotive, personal electronics, communications equipment, enterprise systems, calculators, and others. The company markets and sells its semiconductor products through direct sales and distributors, as well as through its website. Texas Instruments Incorporated was founded in 1930 and is headquartered in Dallas, Texas.

judgment.summary: Profitable semiconductor manufacturer with strong growth and margins, trading at a premium valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 22.8%; next earnings date is 2026-07-28.
positives: Revenue growth 18.6% YoY and earnings growth 31.3% YoY indicate strong business expansion.
Profit margin at 29.1% and operating margin at 37.8% demonstrate excellent operational efficiency.
Return on equity of 32.3% shows effective use of shareholder capital.
Quarterly earnings beat estimates by 22.8% in the last report.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 54.35 and forward P/E of 33.72 suggest a high valuation.
Debt/Equity ratio of 83.74 indicates significant leverage.
Free cash flow of $1.07B is modest relative to market cap and debt.
Dividend payout ratio of 95.0% leaves little room for reinvestment or dividend growth.

news.paragraph: Texas Instruments (TXN) is experiencing a strong upward trend, driven by market recognition of its 300mm capacity potential and robust demand from AI data centers. Recent analyst upgrades, including a price target raise from B of A Securities and positive commentary from Seaport Research, highlight the company's strong Q1 results and optimistic outlook. The narrative suggests TXN is well-positioned to benefit from increasing data center power needs, reinforcing its rally.

headlines.title: Texas Instruments: On A Tear As The Market Realizes The Potential Of 300mm Capacity
Texas Instruments (TXN) Valuation Check After Strong Q1 Results And Growing AI Data Center Demand
TXN Maintained by B of A Securities -- Price Target Raised to $3
Seaport Research Says Rising Data Center Power Needs Could Boost Texas Instruments (TXN)
Texas Instruments Incorporated (TXN) Rallied as Guidance Surpassed Expectations

examples.body: never thought I'd be jumping in on $TXN on a day trade. Peak spiciness.
